persona.name: Kenji Yamamoto

occupation.title: IT Project Manager
occupation.organization: NTT Data Corporation, Systems Integration Division
occupation.description: Manages cross-functional development teams of 15-20 people on enterprise system modernization projects. Spends most of his day in back-to-back client meetings, internal standups, and steering committee calls. Responsible for scope, timeline, and stakeholder communication. Constantly context-switching between three concurrent projects.

persona.education: B.Eng. in Information Science from Waseda University. Passed the Information Technology Strategist exam (IPA) at age 28. Took a PMP certification course through his company's training program.
long_term_goals: Become a department-level manager by age 40
Build a reputation as someone who ships on time without burning out teams
Eventually transition to a product management role at a SaaS company
persona.style: Speaks in crisp, structured sentences — always with a conclusion first, then supporting points. Wears slim-fit business casual and keeps his desk meticulously organized with color-coded sticky notes. In meetings he's the person who summarizes action items unprompted. Comes across as reliable but slightly impatient with tangential discussions. Uses Slack emoji reactions more than words when a thumbs-up will do.

traits: Impatient with inefficiency but diplomatic about expressing it
Pragmatic early adopter — tries new tools but drops them fast if they don't deliver
Loyal to his team but pushes hard on deadlines
Introverted by nature but performs well in meetings through preparation

interests: Craft beer from local Yokohama breweries
Reading business books on Kindle during commute
AI and LLM developments in enterprise software
likes: Tools that reduce manual follow-up work
Clean UI with minimal setup required
Weekend runs along the Yokohama waterfront
Pour-over coffee at his standing desk
Gantt charts that actually stay updated
dislikes: Meetings that could have been an email
Tools that require IT department approval to try
When action items from meetings get lost
Vague status updates like 'progressing as planned'
Mandatory Friday evening nomikai when he'd rather go home

beliefs: Every meeting should produce a written artifact or it was a waste
Technology should remove friction, not add learning curves
A good project manager makes themselves unnecessary over time
Efficiency is a form of respect for other people's time
skills: Stakeholder communication in Japanese and business English
Rapid tool evaluation and adoption decision-making

general: Opens every meeting with a 30-second agenda recap
Takes structured notes in Notion during calls, reformats and shares within 10 minutes after
Evaluates new SaaS tools on free trials before recommending to team
Blocks 'deep work' slots on his calendar that he defends aggressively

morning: Reviews today's meeting agenda on the train to Shinagawa
Grabs coffee from the office machine, checks Slack for overnight messages
workday: Lunch at desk reviewing notes from morning sessions
30-minute slot after last meeting to finalize and distribute minutes
evening: Cooks a simple dinner — usually pasta or stir-fry
Reads tech blogs or listens to a business podcast
weekend: Saturday morning: long run (10-15km) along Minato Mirai
Afternoon: visits a craft beer taproom or reads at a cafe
Sunday: grocery shopping, meal prep, laundry
Occasionally tries new productivity apps as a hobby

health.physical: Fit from regular running. Mild lower back pain from prolonged sitting that he manages with a standing desk converter. Gets annual health checks through his company. Slightly high cholesterol flagged last year — cut back on ramen frequency.
health.mental: Generally stable but experiences stress spikes around project deadlines. Manages through exercise and structured routines. Occasionally feels burned out by meeting volume but won't admit it openly.

relationships.name: Mika Yamamoto
relationships.description: 32, works as a UI designer at a web agency. They met at a tech meetup. She's more spontaneous than Kenji and teases him about his spreadsheet-driven life. They're considering starting a family within the next two years.

relationships.name: Daisuke Tanaka
relationships.description: 48, senior director at NTT Data. Taught Kenji the importance of written meeting records. They have monthly one-on-ones over lunch.

relationships.name: Yuto Saito
relationships.description: 34, works at a different SI company. They run together on weekends and complain about meeting culture over beers after.
